description: Create Firebase Realtime Database security rules (database.rules.json), firebase.json, and .firebaserc. Attempt deployment via Firebase CLI.
steps: Create database.rules.json at repo root with security rules including .read true at root, write validation for contributions and expenses, inline comments explaining the security model.
Create firebase.json that references database.rules.json for database rules.
Create .firebaserc pointing to the Firebase project (use VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID fallback 'demo-project').
Attempt to deploy rules using Firebase CLI (firebase deploy --only database). If auth fails, document that manual deployment is needed.
Commit all changes to a new branch.
acceptance_criteria: database.rules.json exists with proper validation rules and inline comments
Rules include .read: true at root for viewers
Rules include .validate for contributions (memberName string, amount number, date string, id string)
Rules include .validate for expenses (itemName string, cost number, date string, id string, optional purchasedBy)
Inline comments explain the open-write tradeoff and how to add auth gating later
All files committed to a new branch
verification: Validate database.rules.json is parseable JSON (comments must use Firebase rules comment syntax)
Confirm firebase.json and .firebaserc are valid JSON
findings: All three files created successfully. firebase.json and .firebaserc are standard JSON. database.rules.json uses Firebase extended JSON with // comments (supported by Firebase CLI). Deployment attempt failed as expected with 'Failed to authenticate, have you run firebase login?' - this is expected in a CI/sandbox environment without Firebase credentials. Files are version-controlled and ready for manual deployment. App build (npm run build) still passes cleanly.
